# terrible_netapp_ontap_na_ontap_snapshot_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the snapshot policy to be managed. The maximum string length is 256 characters.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `comment` (String) A human readable comment attached with the snapshot. The size of the comment can be at most 255 characters.
- `count` (String) Retention count for the snapshots created by the schedule.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Status of the snapshot policy indicating whether the policy will be enabled or disabled.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `prefix` (String) Snapshot name prefix for the schedule. Prefix name should be unique within the policy. Cannot set a different prefix to a schedule that has already been assigned to a snapshot policy. Prefix cannot be modifed after schedule has been added.
- `retention_period` (String) The retention period of Snapshot copies for the schedule. Valid values are "infinite" or the duration specified in ISO 8601 format. The value when specified in ISO 8061 format for retention period must be in seconds (PT0S - PT65535S), minutes (PT0M - PT60M), hours (PT0H - PT24H), days (P0D - P36500D), months (P0M - P1200M), years (P0Y - P100Y). Supported only with REST and requires ONTAP 9.12 or later.
- `schedule` (String) Schedule to be added inside the policy.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `snapmirror_label` (String) SnapMirror label assigned to each schedule inside the policy. Use an empty string ('') for no label.
- `state` (String) If you want to create, modify or delete a snapshot policy.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vserver` (String) The name of the vserver to use. In a multi-tenanted environment, assigning a Snapshot Policy to a vserver will restrict its use to that vserver.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
